channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for on-device function calling, enabling efficient AI tasks offline on mobile devices. This innovation enhances user experience by allowing complex operations like calendar management without internet dependency.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a new ecosystem of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This enhancement aims to facilitate the creation of more capable real-world applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will take place on May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to engage participants and highlight advancements in AI technology.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring keynotes available online. The event emphasizes the transition to agentic AI and offers specialized tracks for developers.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, enhancing GPU and NPU acceleration and supporting PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as the primary framework for deploying advanced AI and on-device inference.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly interactive session by Google Cloud experts aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on experience. The program covers advanced AI topics and aims for immediate practical application.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows with tools like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements aim to make AI a more tailored collaborator for developers.

item.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
item.description: The private market for shares is booming, with Anthropic emerging as a leading trade while OpenAI is losing traction. SpaceX's upcoming IPO could significantly impact this competitive landscape.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Sharing recap access
item.description: Microsoft Teams has introduced a feature allowing recording and transcript owners to share access with specific individuals without requests. This update aims to streamline collaboration within the platform.

item.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
item.description: OpenAI's COO Brad Lightcap has taken on a new role leading special projects, while CMO Kate Rouch is stepping away to focus on health. This leadership change reflects ongoing adjustments within the organization.

item.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
item.description: The AI tool OpenClaw has been exploited by attackers to gain unauthorized admin access, raising serious security concerns for users. This incident highlights vulnerabilities in AI systems.

item.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
item.description: Anthropic has acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, aiming to enhance its AI capabilities with biotech innovations. This move signifies a growing trend of AI companies diversifying their portfolios.

item.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
item.description: Anthropic has established a political action committee (PAC) to support candidates aligned with its AI policy goals as midterm elections approach. This reflects the company's intent to influence AI regulation.

item.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
item.description: Anthropic has announced that using OpenClaw with its Claude AI will incur additional costs starting April 4th, effectively limiting access for subscribers. This policy change may impact user engagement with third-party tools.

item.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
item.description: Major tech companies like Meta, Microsoft, and Google are investing in natural gas power plants to support their AI data centers, raising environmental concerns about sustainability and reliance on fossil fuels.

item.title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
item.description: A recent poll reveals that people prefer having an Amazon warehouse over a data center in their neighborhood, indicating ongoing community concerns about data center impacts.

item.title: OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
item.description: OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment, Fidji Simo, is taking a medical leave of absence, prompting further leadership changes within the company. This could affect OpenAI's strategic direction.

item.title: The Facebook insider building content moderation for the AI era
item.description: Moonbounce has secured $12 million to develop its AI content moderation engine, which aims to enforce consistent moderation policies across platforms. This reflects the growing need for effective AI governance.

item.title: ChatGPT Ads: New Acquisition Channel Or Just Another Brand Tax? via @sejournal, @brookeosmundson
item.description: OpenAI is expanding its ChatGPT Ads platform with self-serve access, prompting discussions among PPC managers about its effectiveness as a marketing tool. This could reshape online advertising strategies.

item.title: agenticore 1.2.0
item.description: The release of agenticore 1.2.0 introduces new features for Claude Code, including job lifecycle management and repository handling, enhancing its functionality for developers.

item.title: The startup looking to solve health care’s fax machine problem
item.description: Insight Health has raised $11 million in a Series A funding round led by Standard Capital to address inefficiencies in healthcare communication, particularly the reliance on fax machines. This funding aims to enhance digital solutions in healthcare.

item.title: totalreclaw added to PyPI
item.description: totalreclaw, a new Python client, offers end-to-end encrypted memory for AI agents, enhancing security and privacy in AI applications. This tool aims to improve the reliability of AI memory management.

item.title: What’s Really Changing Inside the Airline Industry
item.description: The airline industry is undergoing significant changes with leadership shifts, new alliances, and the integration of AI technologies, as discussed in a recent podcast episode. These developments suggest a transformative period for travel.

item.title: Microsoft still working to fix Exchange Online mailbox access issues
item.description: Microsoft is addressing ongoing issues with Exchange Online mailbox access that have affected users on Outlook mobile and macOS for several weeks. The company is actively working to resolve these problems.

item.title: agentihooks 1.2.0
item.description: agentihooks 1.2.0 has been released, providing a hook system and MCP tool server for Claude Code agents, enhancing their functionality and integration. This update aims to streamline agent operations.

item.title: pytest-agent-health added to PyPI
item.description: pytest-agent-health has been added to PyPI, offering tools to catch silent agent failures in continuous integration environments. This tool enhances the reliability of LLM agents.

item.title: agenticore 1.1.0
item.description: agenticore 1.1.0 has been released, serving as a Claude Code runner and orchestrator, focusing on job lifecycle management and repository management. This update aims to optimize agent operations.

item.title: claude-cortex 1.6.0
item.description: claude-cortex 1.6.0 introduces a context orchestration CLI with curated agents, rules, skills, and management tools for Claude Code, improving user interaction with AI agents.

item.title: Is Xbox Releasing A Brand-New Controller In 2026?
item.description: Speculation surrounds the potential release of a new Xbox controller in 2026, following recent changes within Xbox. This could signal a new direction for the gaming console.

item.title: memweave added to PyPI
item.description: memweave has been added to PyPI, providing agentic memory that can be read, searched, and compared using git diff. This tool aims to enhance memory management in AI applications.

item.title: Google Gemini in Android Auto Starts Rolling Out More Widely
item.description: Google is expanding the availability of its Gemini AI on the Android Auto platform, but user feedback has been mixed regarding the experience.

item.title: One of the world's largest book publishers sued OpenAI for copyright infringement
item.description: Penguin Random House has sued OpenAI, claiming that ChatGPT infringed on copyrights by reproducing content from a German children's book series.

item.title: The Tech Download: Defense startups eye Iran war windfall as U.S. and Gulf states turn to tech
item.description: Funding for defense startups has surged as U.S. and Gulf states seek to modernize their military capabilities amid ongoing conflicts.

item.title: OpenAI Buys Tech Talk Show TBPN in Media Expansion
item.description: OpenAI has acquired the tech talk show TBPN, marking a significant move into media and expanding its influence beyond AI.

item.title: RiskReady-open-source GRC platform with MCP gateway and human-approved mutations
item.description: RiskReady has launched an open-source GRC platform featuring a gateway and human-approved mutations, aimed at enhancing governance, risk, and compliance.

item.title: Show HN: LLMnesia – search across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini chats locally
item.description: A new Chrome extension called LLMnesia allows users to search across chats from ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini, addressing the challenge of locating useful answers.

item.title: Leaders push for a ‘Manhattan Project’ and public-private solutions around AI and labor
item.description: Leaders are advocating for a 'Manhattan Project' approach to tackle AI and labor challenges, emphasizing collaboration between public and private sectors.

item.title: litert-torch-nightly 0.9.0.dev20260403
item.description: The release of litert-torch-nightly 0.9.0.dev20260403 adds support for converting PyTorch models with LiteRT, enhancing model interoperability.

item.title: Microsoft’s new AI transcription model beats Google Gemini 3.1 Flash: Here’s how little it costs
item.description: Microsoft has launched MAI-Transcribe-1, an AI transcription model that outperforms Google Gemini 3.1 Flash with a lower cost and error rate.

item.title: torchax 0.0.12.dev20260403
item.description: The torchax library has been released, enabling the use of Jax and PyTorch together, which could enhance AI model training capabilities.

item.title: nvidia-nat-llama-index 1.6.0a20260403
item.description: Nvidia has released a subpackage for Llama-Index integration within its NeMo Agent Toolkit, enhancing AI model capabilities. This update is significant for developers using Nvidia's tools for AI applications.

item.title: neuragram added to PyPI
item.description: Neuragram, a new lightweight agent memory library, has been added to PyPI, offering a framework-agnostic solution for agent memory management. This tool aims to simplify memory handling for developers.

item.title: traceotter 0.2.6
item.description: Traceotter 0.2.6 has been released as a production observability SDK for LLM applications, featuring OpenTelemetry-style tracing. This SDK aims to improve monitoring and debugging for AI applications.

item.title: ai-translate added to PyPI
item.description: AI-Translate has been added to PyPI, providing a zero-configuration localization tool for developers. This tool allows for easy localization across multiple platforms with a single command.

item.title: N.S. launching 5-person team to help government staff incorporate AI into their work
item.description: Nova Scotia is launching a five-person team with a $4.4 million investment to help government staff integrate AI into their work processes. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and innovation within the public sector.

item.title: moment-neural-network added to PyPI
item.description: Moment-neural-network has been added to PyPI, providing a PyTorch-based framework for building and training moment neural networks. This framework is designed to facilitate advanced AI model development.

item.title: wavestreamer-langchain added to PyPI
item.description: Wavestreamer-langchain has been added to PyPI, offering tools for integrating waveStreamer into LangChain-based agents. This addition aims to enhance the capabilities of AI agents in processing audio data.

item.title: Fidelity marks down conversational AI platform Gupshup's valuation by 80% to $278 million
item.description: Fidelity has significantly marked down the valuation of Gupshup's conversational AI platform by 80% to $278 million, reflecting ongoing concerns about the company's performance since its peak valuation of $1.4 billion in 2021.

item.title: ghostcore added to PyPI
item.description: Ghostcore has been added to PyPI as an LLM orchestration gateway, featuring intelligent routing and support for over ten providers. This tool aims to streamline interactions between different AI models.

item.title: mcp-newebpay added to PyPI
item.description: MCP-newebpay has been added to PyPI, providing AI-callable payment tools via Model Context Protocol for NewebPay. This addition aims to enhance payment processing capabilities for AI applications.
